title: Text Editors: Nano & Vim
content:
### Nano — Simple Terminal Editor

Nano is beginner-friendly with on-screen shortcuts.

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Ctrl+O | Save (WriteOut) |
| Ctrl+X | Exit |
| Ctrl+W | Search |
| Ctrl+K | Cut line |
| Ctrl+U | Paste line |
| Ctrl+G | Help |

```bash
nano file.txt
```

### Vim — Modal Editor (Powerful but Steep)

**Modes:**
| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Normal | Default (navigation and commands) |
| Insert | Type text (press `i` to enter) |
| Visual | Select text (press `v`) |
| Command-line | Ex commands (press `:`) |

**Essential Normal Mode Keys:**

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `i` | Enter insert mode |
| `Esc` | Back to normal mode |
| `dd` | Delete line |
| `yy` | Yank (copy) line |
| `p` | Paste below |
| `u` | Undo |
| Ctrl+r | Redo |
| `/pattern` | Search forward |
| `?pattern` | Search backward |
| `n` / `N` | Next / previous match |

**Saving and Quitting:**

| Command | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `:w` | Save |
| `:q` | Quit |
| `:wq` or `ZZ` | Save and quit |
| `:q!` | Quit without saving |
| `:wq!` | Force save and quit (if readonly) |

**Search and Replace:**

```
:%s/old/new/g          # replace all in file
:5,10s/old/new/g       # replace in lines 5-10
:%s/old/new/gc         # with confirmation
```
codeExample:
nano ~/.bashrc
vim ~/.bashrc
# In vim:
# i to insert, type, Esc, :wq to save and quit
